strengths: Wellâconstructed main research question and subâquestions targeting diagnosis, treatment, and management
Application of the C.R.A.P. test shows mature sourceâevaluation skills ensuring content credibility
Integration of an expert interview adds unique, firstâhand insights beyond secondary sources
Strategic communication skills: thoughtful question design and professional coordination for interviews
Effective selfâmanagement using backward planning, calendars, and Notion to organize time and meet milestones
Structured research findings into tables, improving efficiency and resource management when drafting content
weaknesses: Source evaluation table is comprehensive but would benefit from a column for reliability ratings or bias notes to guide informed selection
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You have applied a **variety of ATL skills** effectivelyâfrom advanced researchâquestion design to meticulous source evaluation and time management. Introducing an explicit **reliability or bias rating** for each source would further strengthen your evidenceâgathering process and ensure that content choices are transparently justified.

strengths: Powerful personalâinterest narrative using vivid family stories to justify your engagement
Wellâjustified choice of a website as the product, highlighting its accessibility and longevity
Success criteria grounded in external research (â7 qualitiesâ article), demonstrating thoughtful definition of effectiveness
Clear backwardâplanning paragraph that links each step to success criteria with strong sequencing rationale
weaknesses: Successâcriteria tables lack exact, measurable thresholds (e.g., âsite loads in <2sâ) to make achievement fully assessable
Learning goal is stated but missing measurable targets (e.g., visitor numbers, awareness metrics) to track progress
Timeline lists tasks and dates but omits explicit resources or interim milestones to guide and monitor workflow
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates **strong personal engagement** and a **thoughtfully sequenced approach**, with wellâjustified criteria rooted in research. To make your plan fully assessable, you should add **specific, measurable targets** and **detailed milestones** (resources, deliverables) so that each success criterion and timeline entry can be clearly evaluated.

strengths: Reflection on refining research methods (e.g., C.R.A.P. tests) demonstrates clear growth in ensuring content credibility
Insightful account of how family feedback led to vocabulary simplification and mobileâusability improvements
Thorough selfâassessment against each success criterion, with clear justification for your evaluations
weaknesses: Reflections lack explicit evidence of how adjustments improved learning outcomes (e.g., knowledgeâcheck comparisons, pre/post tests)
Selfâassigned evaluations would be more robust if supported by userâtesting data or analytics (e.g., bounce rates, navigation success rates)
Absence of specific userâtesting outcomes (survey responses, usability metrics) to validate high achievement levels
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection captures **meaningful personal growth** and **concrete product refinements** driven by feedback. To deepen your evaluation, incorporate **quantitative or qualitative evidence**âsuch as userâtesting results or analyticsâto substantiate your claims and demonstrate the realâworld impact of your improvements.

annotations.comment: Your criteria tables are clear but could be strengthened by adding exact, measurable thresholds (e.g., âsite loads in <2s on mobileâ) to make achievement levels fully assessable.
Your learning goal is clearly stated, but it would benefit from measurable targets (e.g., number of website visitors or awareness posts) to track progress and ensure it is specific and assessable.
Your personal-interest section is powerful, using vivid family narratives to convincingly explain why this topic matters to you and how it motivates your learning.
Your source table is comprehensive, but adding a column to record reliability ratings or bias notes would strengthen your evaluation and guide content selection.
Your reflection on refining research methods (e.g., C.R.A.P. tests) shows clear growth in your ability to ensure content credibility and enhance learning outcomes.
The choice of a website as your product is well justifiedâits accessibility and longevity align clearly with your goal of sustained awareness in the school community.
Grounding your success criteria in external research (the â7 qualitiesâ article) shows a thoughtful approach to defining what makes an effective website.
The backward-plan paragraph crisply explains how each step links to your success criteria and keeps you on track chronologicallyâexcellent sequencing rationale.
Your main research question and sub-questions are well-constructed, targeting diagnosis, treatment, and management to deepen your understanding efficiently.
Applying the C.R.A.P. test to evaluate sources demonstrates mature research skills and ensures your content quality is underpinned by reliable evidence.
Your timeline lists tasks and dates but would be more robust with explicit resources or milestones (e.g., âcomplete content draft using X source by 14 Octâ) to guide your workflow.
Integrating an expert interview enriched your project, providing first-hand insights on genetics and lifestyle changes that go beyond secondary sources.
Your communication-skills paragraph shows strategic question design and professional coordination, key ATL skills for effective knowledge gathering.
Your description of self-management using backward planning, calendars, and Notion exemplifies strong ATL skills in organizing time to meet project milestones.
Organizing research findings into structured tables with categories improved your efficiency when drafting website contentâexcellent resource management.
This reflection describes adjustments but would benefit from explicit evidence of how these changes improved your learning (e.g., knowledge check comparisons or survey results).
Describing how family feedback led you to simplify vocabulary and refine mobile usability demonstrates impactful reflection resulting in concrete product improvements.
Your self-assessment against each success-criterion strand shows thorough evaluation and clear justification for the final grades you awarded.
While your criterion evaluation is detailed, incorporating user-testing feedback or analytics (e.g., bounce rate, load times) would substantiate your self-assigned achievement levels.
To strengthen your evaluation, include specific user-testing outcomes (e.g., survey responses, navigation success rates) to validate your 8/8 ratings against real-world use.
